summary:
TIM-3 has not been validated as an effective therapy in non-small cell lung cancer based on available clinical data. Current standards of care rely exclusively on PD-1/PD-L1 and CTLA-4 inhibitors; thus, TIM-3 blockade lacks sufficient supporting evidence for use in NSCLC.  

Final Answer: \boxed{B}

The summary should be concise and directly address whether TIM-3 qualifies as a valid target within this specific context—NSCLC—and must reflect only what was explicitly reasoned by Agent1 without adding external knowledge.

Only include information from Agent1’s response — do NOT add your own interpretation beyond their stated logic.

TIM-3 is not currently considered a viable target for cancer immunotherapy in NSCLC due to lack of supportive clinical evidence. Standard therapies focus on PD-1/PD-L1 and CTLA-4 pathways, with no demonstrated efficacy or approval for TIM-3 inhibition in this disease. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{B}}$
